intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer dark interfaces for health tracking dashboards to reduce eye strain during frequent check-ins
facade.axisTargeted: Visual presentation — light vs dark, high contrast vs soft
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Build with dark-first design, use low-blue-light palettes, assume frequent evening/night access patterns
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize light/bright interfaces, assume daytime use, consider accessibility for low-vision users who need high contrast light backgrounds

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer dark interfaces for health tracking dashboards to reduce eye strain during frequent check-ins
evidence.implication: Build with dark-first design, use low-blue-light palettes, assume frequent evening/night access patterns

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent left navigation paired with card-based content layout for scanning multiple experiments
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + content organization (sidebar vs tabs; cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick navigation between experiments + digestible, scannable content blocks over dense lists
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tabs, full-width layouts, or list-based organization; dislikes persistent sidebars taking screen real estate

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable interfaces over clinical sharpness
facade.axisTargeted: Roundedness vs. sharpness in visual language
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard uses curves, rounded buttons, soft card edges, friendly aesthetic—feels like a wellness companion not a medical tool
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard uses sharp corners, crisp edges, geometric precision—feels clinical, technical, data-focused rather than nurturing

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard benefits from gradual, flowing color transitions that feel organic and calming rather than stark contrast
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft gradients
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, nature-inspired visual language; prefers smooth transitions over hard edges; likely values contemplative mood over high-contrast urgency
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity through contrast; values clinical precision; leans toward cool or neutral palettes; wants data to feel direct and unambiguous rather than layered

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, animated panel transitions over instant pop-ups when reviewing health experiment data, as motion creates spatial awareness and reduces cognitive friction.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and behavior — animated transitions vs instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth slide-in panels
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard navigation should favor animated reveals, sliding drawers, and eased transitions to create continuity and flow between experiment views.
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard prefers instant, non-animated UI changes — snappy clicks, instant content swap, no transition delay or motion scaffolding.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A friendly, encouraging voice with light personality will feel more motivating for daily health tracking than neutral, clinical language
facade.label: Cheerful check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, motivational tone with personality—consider emoji, casual language, celebratory copy for milestones
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral, clinical, or minimalist tone—remove enthusiasm, use direct language, strip personality markers

facade.hypothesis: Users want to see raw metrics (weight, sleep hours, mood scores) before narrative summaries, to build trust in the data before interpretation
facade.axisTargeted: information hierarchy: quantitative lead vs narrative-first
facade.label: Numbers or story
facade.acceptImplies: Lead with hard data points, show numbers prominently, keep explanatory text secondary, use cards or charts that frontload the metric over insight
facade.rejectImplies: Lead with interpretation and narrative context, bury raw numbers in details, prioritize 'what this means' over 'what happened'

draft.title: Health Experiment Tracker
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for monitoring long-term health experiments with experiment cards, progress metrics, and a simple log interface.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term wellness projects

3
Active
42
Days Avg
Current Experiments

Morning Cold Plunge

3-minute cold water immersion daily

65% complete (26 of 40 days)

Sleep Consistency

Bed by 10pm, wake at 6am

48% complete (19 of 40 days)

Intermittent Fasting

16:8 fasting window, 5 days per week

80% complete (32 of 40 days)
Quick Log
Felt more alert after cold plunge this morning
Today at 8:32 AM
Slept well, woke naturally at 5:55 AM
Yesterday at 6:15 AM
